items.title: Insurers Seek NJ Mass Tort For No Surprises Act Suits - Law360
items.reason: This item reports on litigation strategy related to No Surprises Act enforcement, specifically insurers' attempt to consolidate suits through mass tort proceedings in New Jersey, which directly impacts how NSA disputes are adjudicated.
items.summary: Insurers have sought to establish a mass tort designation in New Jersey for No Surprises Act-related lawsuits, a procedural move that would consolidate multiple suits rather than allowing them to proceed individually. This development is significant for revenue-cycle teams because mass tort consolidation could affect the timeline, scope, and settlement patterns of NSA disputes involving balance billing and network adequacy issues. The attempt to use mass tort procedures reflects the volume of No Surprises Act litigation emerging across states and may influence how healthcare organizations prepare for and respond to NSA enforcement actions.
